title: MatRIS force kernel integration findings
summary: Current generated Triton integration only routes fused_silu, fused_sigmoid, and optional residual_add. Force-related kernels have not been integrated into the real MatRIS force/stress path.

why_energy_to_force_was_not_imported.generated_kernel_semantics: forward(pos) = -2 * pos; backward(grad_out) = -2 * grad_out; double_backward = zeros_like(pos)
why_energy_to_force_was_not_imported.real_matris_force_semantics: force = -d(total_energy)/d(batch_cart_coords), computed by torch.autograd.grad over the full MatRIS energy graph
why_energy_to_force_was_not_imported.reason: The generated energy_to_force kernel is a toy representative for a quadratic energy case, not a general replacement for the real MatRIS force autograd path. Directly importing it would be semantically incorrect.

real_force_path.logic: torch.autograd.grad(total_energy.sum(), [batch_cart_coords, batch_strains], create_graph=is_training, retain_graph=is_training); force = -grad[0]; stress = grad[1] * scale

recommended_next_steps: Add correct small generated kernels for force_negate and stress_scale, replacing only force = -grad[0] and stress scaling.
Regenerate real MatRIS force/stress subgraph kernels rather than using the toy energy_to_force kernel.
Prioritize dynamic-size Dimwise_softmax, aggregate/index_add, scatter/gather kernels because they dominate ef/efs/efsm workloads and force backprop paths.
paper_implication: Current generated activation kernels are insufficient for paper-level speedup on real ef/efs/efsm workloads. Force path and graph aggregation kernels need real integration before reporting positive acceleration results.
